resume.summary: Senior software and infrastructure engineer with 35+ years of experience building and leading teams at Google, Meta, Microsoft, Apple, and PayPal. Inventor of 7 patents — including a Microsoft-patented cloud monitoring and simulation platform — and a hands-on leader of infrastructure, DevOps, and embedded systems programs. Proven track record designing distributed systems, CI/CD pipelines, and fleet management tooling at hyperscale. Equally at home writing low-level C++ and architecting cloud-native Kubernetes platforms.

patents.patent: Patented cloud monitoring and simulation service platform — Microsoft (primary inventor)
6 additional U.S. patents as inventor; 1 as co-inventor — covering cloud infrastructure and distributed systems

highlights.highlight: Championed adoption of Ansible for fleet configuration management; personally authored 95% of the playbooks now governing all HILT device provisioning and state enforcement.
Contributed to hardware design of the team's most complex device under test by designing and 3D-printing a custom mechanical component, improving device useability.
Authored 50% of the team's Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), establishing repeatable processes for device bring-up, fleet management, and incident response.
Led bring-up and integration of multiple complex, in-house designed hardware devices into the team's managed fleet — including physical assembly, OS configuration, and network provisioning.
Developed internal observability and alerting tools in Python/Hack, improving incident detection time and system reliability across enterprise storage infrastructure.
Managed petabyte-scale NetApp and Isilon storage systems serving thousands of internal users, maintaining high availability through proactive capacity planning and on-call support.
Integrated MetaMate and DevMate AI assistants into team workflows, reducing code review cycle time and accelerating feature delivery.
Built automation tooling that scaled Firebase Labs' Android device fleet testing infrastructure, enabling concurrent testing across a significantly larger device pool.
Developed backend services in Python and Go supporting CI/CD pipelines and real-time device fleet monitoring.
Led systems troubleshooting efforts and improved incident response playbooks for GCloud-based infrastructure, reducing mean time to resolution.
Invented a cloud monitoring and simulation service platform that was patented by Microsoft — used for event coordination and observability across Azure services.
Led a proof-of-concept for an Azure-based event service to coordinate messaging across distributed services, later adopted into production architecture.
Provided architecture guidance and Azure best practices to internal teams via the Azure concierge program.
Reduced support ticket response time by 85% through process automation, triage redesign, and runbook development.
Developed SaltStack modules that enforced configuration hygiene across production systems, reducing drift-related incidents.

role.title: Senior Software Engineer
role.summary: 8-year tenure across security, payments, and platform teams. Led projects involving REST APIs, Oracle DB, and multi-language backend services (C++, Java, Python, Ruby). Managed project delivery as technical lead.

role.title: Senior Software Engineer
role.summary: 8-year tenure building carrier-grade telecom software across Unix (AIX, HP-UX, Solaris), Windows, and MVS. Developed with C++, Java Servlets, MQ Series, and WebSphere in a high-availability environment.

highlights.highlight: Development of automated tools for monitoring, management and deployment of horizontally-scaled, highly-available cloud systems
Building performance and reliability metrics in a dynamic application environment
Integrate with engineering teams to provide expertise and requirements

meta.notes: Source HTML at mayelespino.github.io is truncated at the FireEye entry; FireEye details supplied separately by the resume owner (from LinkedIn). No Education section content was present in the source.
